author: @Catknapper
text: Peen plating my aluminum "herb" grinder in copper using pre tumbled seaglass and copper acetate. Opened it up to take a look after 5 minutes and was surprised to see it already coated, yet...somewhat dinged up and dingy looking.

author: @GoogleMapsVids-Asia
text: Ultrasonic with the balls and graphite hmmmmmm

author: @jimsmindonline
text:
You must get driven slightly mad trying to answer everything! ;)
I find Google / scholar slightly irritating when looking for reading, I often end up going through loads of the sites that want you to pay to read!
Tip, adding doctype:pdf to your search helps sometimes.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: i have several subscriptions - so normally i just read the abstract and if it is interesting i pay for it - normally i think i spend a couple of thousand a month on papers - but then i have to or i would lose my lead - but good tip mate - though you do miss out on the latest news if that is a go to position - it might be an idea to do both? and on the answering - it depends on the quality of the question and the number of times i get asked it - i get slightly annoyed when i answer the same question half a dozen times - it makes me wonder why people don't read the comments where i very often have already answered the question they are asking lol

author: @alardiff4104
text: Nows my chance to shine or is that sheen I'm going  to order glass beads thanks for this Rob

author: @craignehring
text:
Perspiration and inspiration... you need both
Well I need both anyways, thank you for providing the inspiration

author: @clixbits
text:
Thanks again for yet another brilliant open source idea.
Maybe you could have a go at creating a water battery using this technique, with magnesium & peen copper plated perforated plastic sheeting. Something along the lines of 'space cowboys' water battery, except with massively increased surface area. Just a thought.(https://www.youtube.com/user/stevensrd1/videos)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: good thought mate - i will have a look at it

author: @Barskor1
text: If you are going to plate a ABS plastic 3D printed object and need to seal it with a wash in acetone why not mix nanoparticles in the acetone as well?

author: @gerardmelvin8670
text: Three great videos .Thanks for sharing your work they are all very helpful and interesting .Happy new year to all who work with you and yourself. Cheers Gez.

author: @nickquik
text: I've always wanted a gold plated peen....

author: @robertphelps41
text:
Robert 

I made a tumbler like that years ago by affixing a washing machine pulley with 3 screws on the end of a Maxwell house can with plastic resealing cover.Then placed it on a pair of old roller skate wheels still mounted on their axles. Found an old v belt and a 12 volt motor, that turned the canister with my brass and walnut shells for fine cleaning. Voila as Frenchie's says you've got a 5 pound tumbler. So how come you over paid.......? Humor and ingenuity make the world good sport ! Ta !

USA Robert

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: nice work mate and they do indeed - it's part of what makes life fun - well for me lol

author: @MrDaniyalAh
text: Can you please make a video on how to make a super capacitor from activated carbon for fish tanks. I am a 10 grade student so please keep it as simple as possible. Your previous video was not clear enough

replies.author: @MrDaniyalAh
replies.text: I am doing it for a science project on a national scale so please help me. I would be very grateful

replies.author: @ACTSRevolution
replies.text: Robert has 100s of videos.  Would you like me to email you the transcripts of his carbon chemistry video comment sections?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: there are lots of videos by others on that mate - i mean lots! - so - really it would just be repeating the work of others and add not very much - i don't have that much time and i try to do new stuff - but it would be good if you watched a few - gave it a go and did a video too
